value.body:
## Expected

When the workspace/global issues page loads issues from multiple repositories, each row should display assignee names and label names/colors for the issue's owning repository. Property menus should also use options from the owning repository.

## Actual

`WorkspaceScreen` passes `repositoryIds` for the global issues page while `repositoryId` is undefined (`src/renderer/screens/WorkspaceScreen.tsx:1086-1088`). `IssuesPanel` then calls `useUserListQuery(repositoryId)` and `useLabelListQuery(repositoryId)` (`src/renderer/components/IssuesPanel.tsx`), but those hooks are disabled when `repositoryId` is undefined (`src/renderer/queries/metadata.ts:124-150`).

As a result, global issue rows can fall back to raw assignee IDs and label IDs, and the assignee/label option lists are built without repository metadata.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Add or open at least two repositories with issues that have assignees and labels.
2. Open the workspace-level/global Issues view.
3. Inspect rows for labeled or assigned issues from multiple repositories.

## Environment

Desktop renderer, global issues page in `packages/desktop`.

## Suggested solution

Add metadata loading for the global `repositoryIds` path. Fetch users and labels per repository, keep maps keyed by repository id, and pass the owning repository's metadata into `issueRow` / property controls using `repositoryIdForIssue(issue, repositoryId)`. This keeps global issue rows readable and avoids offering assignee/label options from the wrong repository.

frontmatter.title: Global issues view shows raw assignee and label IDs

value.title: Global issues view shows raw assignee and label IDs
